description:
India Book I, 15: Three testimonies regarding collateral deposited by Yosef al-Lebdi the India trader. Lebdi owed Ḥasan b. Bundar, the representative of the merchants in Aden, 40 dinars for an indigo deal. Until Lebdi could pay the debt in full, he had to deposit collateral, probably because he was a foreigner who was expected to leave town. The first and last of these three entries are written and signed by Netan’el b. Yefet.
The first is signed by Netanel, Yiṣḥaq b. Shemu'el and Eli ha-Kohen b. Yaḥya. The last is signed by Netanel and Yiṣḥaq b. Shemu'el.
This is the recto of the second leaf of CUL Add.3420. The first leaf contains document I, 3 (PGPID 5389) and the verso of this page is document I, 8 (PGPID 5393). 
Goitein referred to this document as CUL Add.3420c.
description_he: ספר הודו I,15: שלוש עדויות על בטוחות שהפקיד יוסף אל-לבדי בגין חוב של 40 דינר לחסן בן בונדאר
